betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Boiler§f

§aHeat crude oil to §e350°C§f so it can be refined — this is a mandatory step before the Oil Refinery!§f

The Boiler is an essential machine that heats crude oil (and other fluids) to high temperatures. It requires an external heat source like a §bFirebox§f placed directly underneath. Crude oil must be heated to become §bHot Crude Oil§f before the §bOil Refinery§f can process it.

§6Crafting§f:
Crafted on an §bAnvil§f (Tier 2, Steel or above) using §e4× Steel Ingot§f, §e16× Copper Plate§f, and §e8× Insulator§f.

§6Operation§f:
Place a §bFirebox§f or similar heat source and position the boiler on top. Use a §bFluid Identifier§f by §9right-clicking§f the boiler to select which fluid to heat — it defaults to water.
§b• Input Tank§f: §e16,000mB§f capacity.
§b• Output Tank§f: Corresponding size (depends on fluid expansion when heated).
§b• Heat Transfer§f: ΔT ⋅ §e0.01 TU/t§f.
§b• Duct Ports§f: Three §bUniversal Fluid Duct§f connections — one on top, two on sides.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Crude Oil§f: §e5mB§f crude oil → §e5mB§f hot crude oil (required for the Oil Refinery).
§b• Water§f: Boil water into §bSteam§f for power generation.
§b• Cracked Oil / Desulfurized Oil§f: Also heatable before refining.

§cWarning§f: The boiler will §cexplode§f if the output tank overflows! Monitor fluid levels carefully. The §bIndustrial Boiler§f does not explode.

§eTip§f: Many refinery setups include a §bFluid Burner§f that burns low-grade fuel (like §bHeating Oil§f) to heat the boiler, making the system self-sustaining as long as crude oil flows in.
